stories.headline: Zelenskyy asks Trump to send missiles after Russian strikes across Ukraine
stories.summary: At least 18 killed, dozens injured and others trapped under collapsed buildings after attacks on five Ukrainian citiesVolodymyr Zelenskyy has asked Donald Trump to send Patriot missiles to Ukraine after a devastating Russian attack killed at least 18 people and injured dozens more.Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine overnight, according to the air force, including eight hypersonic Tsirkon missiles. The main targets were Kyiv, th

stories.headline: Russia may launch another large scale attack against Ukraine tonight, Zelenskyy warns
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy issued a stark warning on Tuesday evening saying that according to Ukraine’s intelligence Russia may launch another large-scale aerial attack tonight. It comes as Moscow is exploiting a critical shortage of air defence interceptor missiles.

stories.headline: Trump taps housing official Bill Pulte to be US spymaster
stories.summary: The head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, who pushed for criminal charges against Trump's foes, will step in as acting Director of National Intelligence.

stories.headline: Ukraine rescuers pull dead from rubble after Russian strikes kill 22 people
stories.summary: An eight-year-old boy killed in a strike on an apartment block are among the dead, officials say.

stories.headline: Trump appoints real estate heir Bill Pulte as national intelligence chief
stories.summary: Housing industry executive and staunch ally of the president to take up role in acting capacity

stories.headline: What to watch in Tuesday’s US primary elections
stories.summary: Votes from California to Iowa offer gauge of Americans’ mood and their appetite for more Maga

stories.headline: Why the U.S.-Iran Negotiations Are Taking So Long
stories.summary: As negotiations to end the war in Iran drag on, our reporter David E. Sanger describes the factors that are complicating any agreement.

stories.headline: Trump Names Bill Pulte as Acting Director of National Intelligence
stories.summary: Bill Pulte, a Trump loyalist who leads the federal housing finance agency, will oversee the U.S. intelligence agencies. He has no known background in intelligence.

stories.headline: What to Watch in Tuesday’s Primary Elections in Iowa, Montana and Beyond
stories.summary: Democrats have a competitive Senate primary race in Iowa, where the party is hoping to make gains, while a smokejumper is running in a contested House primary in Montana.

stories.headline: Magyar teases imminent deal on Hungarian minority to lift veto on Kyiv's EU accession
stories.summary: Magyar says he is "very optimistic" about a deal on minority rights in Ukraine after years of political impasse. An agreement between Budapest and Kyiv on the delicate matter would allow for EU accession talks to begin.

stories.headline: Spain bans DR Congo World Cup warm-up due to Ebola fears
stories.summary: Spanish mayor bans DR Congo pre-World Cup 2026 game in La Linea de la Concepcion due to Ebola fears.

stories.headline: Why is the EU establishing return hubs for migrants — and why are they so controversial?
stories.summary: The new migration law approved on Monday allows EU countries to establish return centres outside the bloc. But critics warn it could lead to human rights violations, while governments are seeking partner countries to host the facilities.

stories.headline: France detains tanker linked to Iranian network accused of moving Russian oil
stories.summary: The seizure is the latest evidence of France's efforts to crack down on vessels suspected of helping Russia evade sanctions imposed following Moscow's invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: British couple lose Iran jail sentence appeal, family says
stories.summary: Lindsay and Craig Foreman were arrested in January 2025 during a global motorbike journey.

stories.headline: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen to form government after months of negotiations
stories.summary: The leader of Denmark's Social Democratic Party is forming a centre-left coalition minority government, giving her a third term as prime minister.

stories.headline: Clashes continue in Lebanon despite Israel and Hezbollah accepting US partial ceasefire plan
stories.summary: A cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah is seen as crucial to the peace process with Iran.

stories.headline: Microsoft targets Anthropic with new model releases
stories.summary: Software giant’s AI chief Mustafa Suleyman says focus is on developing products for business users

stories.headline: Middle Market Businesses 'Real Driver of Economy' Says Wendy Stewart
stories.summary:
Wendy Stewart, president of Global Commercial Banking at Bank of America, said that middle market businesses are driving the economy right now, driving growth and hiring and firing amid an uncertain economic environment. 
Stewart also said that global supply chains still exist, but that businesses have to be flexible given the geopolitical landscape.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Rubio to Testify Before Senate, Arguing for Budget Amid Iran War and Pressure on Cuba
stories.summary: The secretary of state has played a key role in the Iran deliberations, as well as in U.S. policy toward Cuba.

stories.headline: California Primary Elections: What to Watch in the Governor’s Race, L.A. Mayoral Race and More
stories.summary: The race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom has been unpredictable for months, while Mayor Karen Bass of Los Angeles is trying to fend off two challengers.

stories.headline: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa

stories.headline: Politics live: Australia always preferred secondhand Aukus submarines, defence secretary says

stories.summary: Joy turned to panic as Sarah and her dog sank quickly. stories.summary: Social Democrat stays on for third consecutive term as PM and will outline key policies by the end of TuesdayDenmark’s new government is preparing to formally present its political programme after Mette Frederiksen negotiated a third consecutive term as prime minister, this time at the head of a four-party, left-leaning minority coalition.Announced late on Monday, the agreement between Frederiksen’s Social Democrats, the Social Liberals, the Gree

stories.headline: What to know about Tuesday’s primaries in California, New Jersey, Montana
stories.summary: Voters in six US states are casting primary ballots that will set the battle lines for November's midterm elections.

stories.headline: US targets Brazil with new tariffs over trade practices
stories.summary: The US plans new 25 percent tariffs on Brazilian imports, citing issues like deforestation and digital trade practices.

stories.headline: Two people shot dead in Kenya protests against Ebola plan
stories.summary: Kenyan police shot and killed two people who were protesting a US-backed plan to establish an Ebola quarantine facility in central Kenya.

stories.headline: Mysteriously missing New Jersey congressman receives Trump endorsement
stories.summary: Republican Congressman Tom Kean Jr has not been seen for months but is expected to win Tuesday's election.

stories.headline: Canada formally requests 16-year renewal of North American free trade pact
stories.summary: In a letter to his North American counterparts, US-Canada Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said the three countries should renew the USMCA trade deal.

stories.headline: South Africa police investigate killing of two Mozambican men
stories.summary: The deaths come after a day of violent protests against illegal migration in the town of Mossel Bay.

stories.headline: Ghana's anti-LGBTQ+ bill to be scrutinised before approval, president says
stories.summary: Passed by MPs, the bill proposes prison sentences for people identifying as part of the LGBTQ+ community.

stories.headline: Australian ex-minister launches crowd-funded inquiry into Aukus submarine deal
stories.summary: Inquiry will look at whether Australia will receive the submarines and whether it will make the country safer.

stories.headline: Why Myanmar president's India visit is being closely watched
stories.summary: Myanmar is seeking to broaden diplomatic engagement after years of international criticism and isolation.

stories.headline: China goes after 'ghost kitchens' to rein in cut-throat food delivery apps
stories.summary: The thousands of "ghost kitchens" - online shops that don't actually exist - have spooked Chinese consumers.

stories.headline: US convertible bonds set for record year as issuers harness AI boom
stories.summary: Investors embrace zero-interest debt for options on high-growth tech stocks

stories.headline: BP held talks about selling £2bn of UK North Sea assets to Ithaca
stories.summary: Oil major still exploring a deal despite failed discussions as CEO Meg O’Neill pursues restructuring

stories.headline: How China’s Wealthy Sidestep Strict Rules to Get Money Out of the Country
stories.summary: China’s capital controls remain among the world’s strictest. Individuals are generally limited to transferring $50,000 overseas each year, while emigrants are given a one-time opportunity to move their assets abroad.

stories.headline: Canadian Stocks Index Hits 35,000 Amid Rally in Energy
stories.summary: The Canadian equities benchmark passed 35,000 for the first time on a broadening rally in energy and financials.

stories.headline: Short-Sellers Are Dug In as Bond Market Faces Big Payrolls Test
stories.summary: Bond traders are locked in to wagers on higher yields even after paring some of their more extreme positions, with critical US employment data due on Friday that may add to the argument for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes.

stories.headline: UBS Sells $1.5 Billion AT1, First Since Swiss Reform Pause
stories.summary: UBS Group AG raised $1.5 billion of Additional Tier 1 capital in US dollars, its first foray into the market since Switzerland paused plans to tighten rules on the riskiest type of bank debt.

stories.headline: Venezuela Moves to Open Power Sector to Private Investment
stories.summary: Venezuela’s National Assembly will debate a reform that would open the country’s electricity sector to private investment, allowing companies to generate, distribute and sell power under government concessions after more than 15 years of state control.

stories.headline: Traders Most Bullish Yuan in 15 Years on Global Role, Valuation
stories.summary: Traders ramped up wagers on a stronger yuan in the coming year, betting that increased global usage and an attractive valuation will guide China’s currency through near-term speed bumps.

stories.headline: Inside the billion-dollar business of getting a visa
stories.summary: An investigation reveals how visa giant VFS Global profits from millions of visa applications from the Global South.

stories.headline: China opens its markets to African exports. Who benefits?
stories.summary: China's tariff-free access for many African products has renewed hopes of industrial growth. But trade remains uneven.

stories.headline: Intisar Shanib becomes first woman to head football club in Libya
stories.summary: Shanib has been appointed as the president of Darnes Sports Club, a prominent football club in Libya's Derna city.

European Commission is set to criticise Italy for weak industrial strategy
stories.summary: The Commission will criticise Italy’s lack of robust industrial plans and calls for measures to incentivise investment in capital markets as well as reform of tax system, in country-specific recommendations to be published tomorrow.

stories.headline: Southern energy corridors set to dominate supplies to Europe, global leaders say
stories.summary: The rapid reshape of global energy supply routes and markets is a key focus at the Baku Energy Week 2026.

stories.headline: Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down
stories.summary: Under pressure from the polls and Gulf allies, the White House is pushing for a deal but Iran wants concessions, writes BBC's international editor.

stories.headline: Iran attacks damage 20 US military sites since start of war, satellite images show
stories.summary: Analysts told BBC Verify that Tehran's strikes were more extensive than publicly acknowledged.

stories.headline: S&P 500 Tops 7,600 as AI Fuels Nine-Day Win Streak: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A revival of the artificial-intelligence trade kept driving Wall Street momentum, with stocks also rising on hopes for an agreement that would end the war that has roiled financial markets around the world.

stories.headline: People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity

stories.headline: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
stories.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.

stories.headline: Huge blast kills dozens in rebel-held village in Myanmar
stories.summary: Insurgents say it was caused by explosives being used for mining close to the Chinese border.

stories.headline: Gold Pares Gains as US Jobs Data Reinforces Higher-Rate Bets
stories.summary: Gold pared gains after robust US jobs data reinforced bets that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates higher for longer, a headwind for the precious metal.

stories.headline: Oil Swings Higher as Traders Weigh Prospects of US-Iran Deal
stories.summary: Oil edged higher as traders parsed discordant signals on the prospects of a US-Iran peace deal.

stories.headline: Japan defence minister denies militarism and criticises China's 'huge arsenal'
stories.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi makes some of Tokyo's most pointed remarks yet in the dispute over Japan's military build-up.

stories.headline: Secret tunnels and unregistered workers: China's coal mine disaster is a reminder of darker days
stories.summary: China's worst coal mining disaster in 15 years comes amid an ambitious pivot towards green energy.

stories.headline: Caribbean hot sauce producers warn of shortages and higher prices
stories.summary: Manufacturers in Jamaica say the key chilli peppers they need are in limited supply.

stories.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi says Japan valued as a ‘peace-loving’ nation while China expands military capabilities ‘without sufficient transparency’Japan’s defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing’s criticism of Tokyo’s increasingly muscular security stance.Under the prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, Japan has quickened its pivot to a more proactive defence policy, further shaking off

stories.headline: How Putin became master of the image
stories.summary: From enigmatic KGB agent to wartime ruler, this is how Putin has repeatedly reinvented his image, and himself.

stories.headline: Ecuador accused of meddling in Colombian election with tariff vow
stories.summary: Ecuadorian leader Daniel Noboa offers to cancel tariffs after speaking to a right-wing presidential candidate ahead of Sunday's vote.

stories.headline: Netanyahu says he has directed IDF to increase control of Gaza to 70%
stories.summary: The expansion in control by Israel would contradict the terms of the ceasefire  Israel and Hamas agreed to in October 2025.

stories.headline: Israel put on UN sexual violence in warzones blacklist for first time
stories.summary: Israel says it rejects allegations in a new report by the office of the UN secretary general.

stories.headline: US and Iran 'very close' to deal but 'not there yet', Vance says
stories.summary: US officials earlier told the BBC that the framework of a ceasefire extension deal had been agreed, pending the approval of Trump and Iran's leadership.

stories.headline: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders
stories.summary: At least five children were reportedly killed in the strike, which appears to have targeted a Hamas commander.

stories.headline: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
stories.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

stories.headline: How Cuba is addressing its housing crisis with shipping containers
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant visited Barrio Toledo, where at least 700 containers are being repurposed into two-bedroom homes with kitchens, bathrooms and patios.

stories.headline: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
stories.summary: Hamas says Mohammed Odeh was killed alongside his wife and two children in a strike on a residential building.
